plan.reasoning: SMILES [CH2]C represents an ethyl radical (CH2-CH3). From the RDKit analysis: atom index 0 is C (SP3, radical_electrons=1, degree=1) and atom index 1 is C (SP3, radical_electrons=0, degree=1). The radical center is at index 0 (the CH2 group), making this a ReactiveSpecies. Since index 0 has an unpaired electron, it is the binding atom. For the first attempt with no history, I will place the radical carbon (index 0) at an ontop Hf site, as Hf is the most electropositive metal on the surface and typically forms strong bonds with carbon radicals. This is a single binding point (ontop), consistent with len(adsorbate_binding_indices)==1. Surface composition includes Hf, Sb, Zn; ontop Hf is available.
